mechanism: Non-reversing tariff escalation progressively raises import costs through direct price pass-through and retaliatory spirals, creating a ratcheting floor under tradeable goods inflation
regime_dependence: Holds under bipartisan protectionist convergence; would weaken if political off-ramp emerges or GDP contraction forces reversal (1/5 historical base rate)

mechanism: Supply-side inflation from deglobalization increases supply-shock variance relative to demand-shock variance, flipping stock-bond correlation positive via Campbell-Sunderam-Viceira framework
regime_dependence: Holds when supply-shock variance dominates; correlation reverts negative when central bank easing or demand shocks dominate (oscillating regime)

mechanism: Inflation variance from tariff policy discontinuity and supply chain tail risk elevates compensation demanded by Treasury investors; operates through affine term structure models where term premium compensates for inflation uncertainty
regime_dependence: Holds as long as tariff policy discontinuity persists; would moderate if trade policy stabilizes

mechanism: Ratcheting tariff escalation pushes effective tariff rates toward and beyond the 15-25% nonlinear threshold where pass-through jumps from 30-50% to 60-90%, creating a regime break in inflation and credit transmission
regime_dependence: The nonlinear threshold is relatively stable; the relationship activates when cumulative tariff ratchet crosses the ~15-25% effective rate zone

mechanism: Massive Chinese overcapacity in specific sectors (solar, EVs, batteries, steel) suppresses global prices and absorbs 20-50% of tariff costs, dampening inflationary pass-through. But offset fails in sectors without overcapacity (semis, pharma, aerospace, specialty chemicals).
regime_dependence: Sector-specific, not aggregate. Works only where Chinese overcapacity exists and tariffs don't fully block imports. Breaks if enforcement blocks the deflationary transmission

mechanism: Highest-severity supply chain vulnerability has near-zero premium in IG industrial spreads despite demonstrable weaponization pattern; cross-silo opacity prevents any single risk model from integrating this exposure
regime_dependence: Underpricing persists until either a disruption event reprices risk or supply chain transparency improves; the 5-10 year vulnerability window overlaps with peak geopolitical risk

mechanism: The 5-10 year window to build alternative rare earth processing capacity defines the minimum duration of the transition vulnerability; overlaps with peak Taiwan contingency risk period
regime_dependence: Structural — no policy acceleration can close the processing gap faster than the physical/chemical engineering constraints allow

mechanism: Cosmetic friendshoring inflates headline announced capex by counting trade rerouting as genuine relocation; enforcement tightening will reveal the gap between announced and actual investment
regime_dependence: Gap widens when enforcement is lax and corporations choose 3-8% cosmetic options; narrows as rules-of-origin enforcement tightens

mechanism: Joint stress of refinancing (40-60% higher costs) and margin compression (150-400bp lower operating margins) creates correlated defaults that push outcomes toward the adverse mode (7-9%) rather than the benign mode (2.5%)
regime_dependence: Activates when maturity wall peak (2025-2028) coincides with effective tariff rates above the 15-25% nonlinear threshold; benign mode prevails if tariffs stay below threshold or maturities are extended

mechanism: Thinning offshore dollar liquidity from trade surplus recycling decline amplifies financial stress during the transition window when supply chains are partially duplicated and capital is committed to incomplete projects
regime_dependence: Amplification strongest during active de-dollarization and geopolitical stress; Fed swap lines can partially mitigate for allied nations but not for adversary or non-aligned nations

mechanism: Contradictions between the three deglobalization processes (security, mercantilist, corporate) create incentives for cosmetic compliance: mercantilist tariffs on allied suppliers undermine security-driven friendshoring, pushing corporations toward the cheapest cosmetic diversion
regime_dependence: Contradiction is structural — exists whenever multiple policy objectives conflict. Resolves only if one logic dominates (e.g., national security overrides cost optimization)

mechanism: Both driven by the same underlying force (supply-shock dominance) and mutually reinforce: positive correlation reduces bond diversification value, requiring higher term premium to attract investors; elevated term premium reflects the inflation uncertainty that drives positive correlation
regime_dependence: Co-directional under supply-shock dominance; both reverse under demand-shock dominance or successful central bank anchoring of expectations

mechanism: Uncertainty about which inflation mode will materialize (60% price-level shift vs 40% persistent regime shift) directly elevates term premium because investors cannot resolve the distribution and demand compensation for this ambiguity
regime_dependence: Holds while the bimodal outcome remains unresolved; term premium would narrow if leading indicators clearly favor one mode

mechanism: 50-70% completion rates and systematic delays (TSMC Arizona, Intel Ohio) extend the transition window beyond planned timelines, prolonging the period of maximum vulnerability
regime_dependence: Execution gap is structural given workforce constraints (500,000+ additional manufacturing workers needed) and project complexity; partially mitigated by national security political persistence

mechanism: Subsidy-dependent firms suppress measured default rates and tighten aggregate spreads, masking non-subsidized deterioration and making aggregate credit metrics unreliable indicators of true risk
regime_dependence: Persists as long as subsidies are disbursed; fiscal tightening or political reversal would expose the masked deterioration

mechanism: Tier 2 EMs (Malaysia, Thailand, Indonesia) serve as primary conduits for cosmetic trade diversion; transshipment crackdowns primarily target this tier, creating vulnerability that Tier 1 (India, Poland) avoids through deeper genuine integration
regime_dependence: Tier assignments shift with enforcement intensity — aggressive rules-of-origin enforcement could demote Tier 2 countries or force genuine investment that promotes them

mechanism: Crossing the 15-25% tariff threshold triggers accelerated margin compression for import-dependent issuers, amplifying refinancing stress by degrading coverage ratios precisely when borrowers need to roll maturing debt
regime_dependence: Activates specifically when effective tariff rates cross the nonlinear threshold; below threshold, firms absorb costs and coverage ratios remain manageable

mechanism: Deglobalization regime increases loadings on value factor as tangible-asset-heavy domestic firms benefit relative to asset-light global supply chain firms; the tailwind is a factor loading shift, not an independent alpha source
regime_dependence: Active during deglobalization regime; would reverse if re-globalization occurs or if AI-driven intangible premium offsets tangible-asset advantage

mechanism: Historical pattern (0/2 base rate for system centrality preservation) suggests weaponization of dollar system accelerates development of alternatives, gradually thinning the offshore dollar funding base that serves as crisis transmission mechanism
regime_dependence: Long-run (20-40 year) relationship; near-term dollar dominance persists but marginal thinning increases crisis-time fragility
